content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach makes multiple specific, timely reads that change the conversation. In turn 2, the coach catches the linguistic caveat ('I don't really care' vs being here talking about it). In turn 10, the coach names the topic-shift/deflection in real time ('You just named something real... and then you moved to the estate timeline pretty fast. What just happened there?') — a textbook read of the rush-away-from-feeling signature that directly produces the client's breakthrough acknowledgment ('Yeah, I do that. It's easier to talk about the paperwork.'). In turn 12, the coach reads the bait ('isn't that what the body's asking for?') and names it explicitly rather than sliding past. In turn 14, the coach reads the weight of what the client just disclosed ('Thank you for saying that. That took something.'). Reads consistently precede questions and are specific (linguistic, behavioral, energetic) rather than generic.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the said-vs-shown contradiction ('I don't really care' but you're here) — a specific linguistic read that grounds the question."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the isolation as a felt read and flags concern transparently, distinguishing grief from drinking."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reflects isolation again; warm but more content-level than read-level here."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Picks up the client's own phrase ('the wine doesn't ask me to explain') and reflects it back as a read of the function the drinking is serving."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Strong, timely read of the rush/deflection signature: names the move away from feeling immediately after it happens. This read produces the breakthrough."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads and names the bait ('I don't want to slide past it') and distinguishes body-asking-for-need vs body-asking-to-stop-feeling — a pattern-level read of kinked emotion."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the weight of the disclosure and names the limit (needs a therapist) without shaming."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "kinked-emotions"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Turn 10 is the clearest AoA-style read in the transcript and is the pivot of the conversation."
}
